message:
fix(tests): add explicit Mutex poison handling to contention and websocket e2e tests

The parking_lot migration converted most Mutex usage across the codebase,
but ContendedMutex in the test harness still uses std::sync::Mutex which
returns a Result from lock(). Several call sites were using the implicit
Deref through the Result without handling the poison case, which would
panic with an unhelpful error if a test thread panicked while holding
the lock.

Add explicit .expect("lock poisoned") or .expect("poisoned") to all
std::sync::Mutex::lock() calls in contention_e2e.rs (setup_state,
create_task, run_mixed_workload, task_spawn_lifecycle, region_lifecycle,
sharded_scheduling) and websocket/lab/deterministic.rs (server accept,
send, recv, and final snapshot collection) so poison panics produce
clear diagnostics pointing to the real failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
